## vvx
Job Jockey Kit - MCP tools for project initiative management

attachment.content:
- cma-prep-pr: Prepare branch for upstream PR contribution
- rbk-prep-release: Prepare release candidate for upstream delivery
- rbk-wrangle: Muster, then roster RBK and JJK heats as two space-padded plain-text blocks
- vvc-BREAK-LOCK: Force-break a stuck VVC lock after a crash.
- vvc-commit: Guarded git commit
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

## Paces

### antipattern-catalog-broaden (₢BbAAH) [rough]

## Character
Small MCM/ACG/JJK cross-document curation — one definitional broadening, three quoin mints, citer repointing.

## Goal
Make MCM's Antipatterns section the single catalog for named recurring defects,
document-side and delivery-side alike;
home Stun, Molehill, and word cancer there as quoins;
flip every defining citer to cite.

## Cinched
- mcm_antipattern's definition broadens from document defect to also cover
  delivery defects (operator-facing presentation failures) — the junk-drawer
  election, operator-cinched at groom.
- Word cancer mirrors the tidemark pattern: concept home is the MCM quoin;
  ACG keeps the moves (statements clause, ACGm_105, registry) and cites.
- Stun and Molehill keep their operational guidance in the JJK
  gradient-delivery section (claude-jjk-bhyslop.md), which cites the MCM home
  instead of defining.

## Done when
MCM Antipatterns carries the broadened definition plus quoins for word cancer,
Stun, and Molehill; ACG and the JJK gradient-delivery guidance cite rather than
define; MCM validates.

### pilot-audit-sentry (₢BbAAI) [rough]

## Character
First ACG audit pilot — detect-only judgment work; the guide is on trial as much
as the documents.

## Gate
Top-tier model only (Fable-class at slating), worked in the operator's chat —
never mount or delegate this pace at a lesser tier.
The pilot's primary deliverable is ACG amendment judgment — noticing where the
guide is vague, wrong, or silent — and delegation makes that feedback
secondhand.
If mounted on a lesser tier, stop and tell the operator instead of proceeding.

## Goal
Audit the sentry pair — Tools/rbk/rbjs_sentry.sh against RBSSR + RBSSS — under
ACG's detect-only moves (ACGm_104 conceptual-comment eviction, ACGm_106 ornament
drain; note ACGm_105/107 findings in passing for the corpus sweeps).
Twin deliverable: the findings report,
and ACG amendments wherever the guide proved vague, wrong, or silent —
pilots harden the guide.

## Cinched
- Per-pair shape: source side and spec side read together; a relocation's
  receiving quoin is judged against this pair's spec.
- Detect-only licensing per ACG: report findings; relocations land one reviewed
  move at a time, never batch.
- This pair's dense security-rationale comments are the deliberate test of the
  edit-time/Palisade boundary — whether why-comments at the iptables/conntrack
  sites are evictable design-time knowledge or legitimate Palisade
  characterization. Settle with the operator; record the ruling as an ACG
  amendment.

## Done when
Findings report exists with each finding tagged by move-type and receiving home;
agreed relocations/drains landed as individually reviewed moves;
ACG amendments landed;
fast suite green if any source file changed.

### pilot-audit-access-probes (₢BbAAJ) [rough]

## Character
Second ACG audit pilot — prose-judgment focus; runs against known-positive
specimens.

## Gate
Top-tier model only (Fable-class at slating), worked in the operator's chat —
never mount or delegate this pace at a lesser tier.
The pilot's primary deliverable is ACG amendment judgment — noticing where the
guide is vague, wrong, or silent — and delegation makes that feedback
secondhand.
If mounted on a lesser tier, stop and tell the operator instead of proceeding.

## Goal
Audit the access-probe pair — Tools/rbk/rbgv_AccessProbe.sh against
RBSAJ + RBSAO — under ACG's detect-only moves, with emphasis on ACGm_105
(paraphrase collapse) and ACGm_106 (ornament drain):
the groom survey found spec and source wording the bounded
propagation-absorbing-retry idea twice,
and rationale-as-normative prose in RBSAJ.
Twin deliverable: findings plus ACG amendments.

## Cinched
- The known specimens serve as ACG rule-2 known answers: if the audit misses
  them, the detect rule is wrong — fix the guide, not the report.
- Triage rider: Manor/Payor/account-role vocabulary is ₣BZ's lane — report,
  never touch.
- Word-cancer findings cite the MCM antipattern home established by the
  preceding antipattern-catalog pace.

## Done when
Findings report with move-type tags;
agreed collapses/drains landed as individually reviewed moves,
one authoritative wording per idea with citations elsewhere;
ACG amendments landed;
fast suite green if any source file changed.

### pilot-audit-conjure (₢BbAAK) [rough]

## Character
Third ACG audit pilot — the scale test on the richest surface; multi-module
pairing.

## Gate
Two gates, both hard:

- Top-tier model only (Fable-class at slating), worked in the operator's chat —
  never mount or delegate this pace at a lesser tier.
  The pilot's primary deliverable is ACG amendment judgment plus the roster
  recommendation, and delegation makes that feedback secondhand.
  If mounted on a lesser tier, stop and tell the operator instead of proceeding.
- After ₣BH (lode-universal-capture) lands — the conjure surface is under
  active ₣BH rewrite (ensconce cutover, anchor wiring);
  findings against it now go stale.

## Goal
Audit the conjure pair — Tools/rbk/rbfd_FoundryDirectorBuild.sh (plus its
rbfca_/rbfcg_/rbfcp_ support where the spec reaches) against RBSAC — under the
full detect-only move set.
This pilot answers whether the per-pair shape scales:
biggest source surface, deep conceptual comments
(anchor semantics, egress-mode branching, two-layer preflight).
Twin deliverable: findings plus ACG amendments,
closing with a recommendation for the post-pilot groom —
roster of pair paces vs further pilots.

## Cinched
- Runs only after the first two pilots' ACG amendments have landed — this pilot
  exercises the hardened guide.
- Reliquary/Lode/Hallmark vocabulary belongs to ₣BH's lane even after it lands;
  term curation stays out of scope — allocation findings only.

## Done when
Findings report with move-type tags;
agreed moves landed as individually reviewed moves;
ACG amendments landed;
roster recommendation recorded for the post-pilot groom;
fast suite green if any source file changed.

### rbrv-env-bash-sweep (₢BbAAE) [rough]

## Character
Mechanical sweep gated on one small design fork at its head — the first worked
application of ACG's magic-string discipline.

## Goal
Give the rbrv.env filename literal a single named home in bash; sweep the
path-construction sites onto it.

## Head fork (settle at mount)
Does the constant reference the existing rbrv_regime quoin (RBS0) or a freshly
minted filename quoin distinct from the regime? The regime-vs-file split is real
— sourcing/validation act on the regime; yoke/inscribe/enumerate act on the file
— so lean to a distinct filename quoin, but confirm against MCM "warrants
anchoring".

## Gate
After ₣BH (lode-universal-capture) lands. This sweep's path-construction sites
overlap BH's active rewrite surface — the inscribe cutover repoints the yoke and
Director-track rbrv.env handling, and BH's terminal vocab scrub greps the whole
tree last — so landing this in a parallel repo before BH's tail would guarantee a
merge conflict. Run it after BH is done, in the main line.

## Locked
- Constant joins the RBCC_rbr*_file family in rbcc_Constants.sh (sibling to
  RBCC_rbrn_file / rbra_file / rbro_file), with a breadcrumb to its spec quoin.
- Sweep only genuine path constructions. Discover: grep -rn "rbrv\.env" Tools/rbk
  --include="*.sh"; exclude prose — doc_params, buc_info/buh_line messages, and
  comments keep the literal (naming the file for a human is not a magic string).

## Done
Every bash path-construction resolves through the constant; prose literals
untouched; the constant carries its quoin breadcrumb; tt/rbw-tl.Shellcheck.sh and
tt/rbw-ts.TestSuite.fast.sh green.

### rbrv-env-rust-sweep (₢BbAAF) [rough]

## Character
Mechanical — RCG Constant Discipline: dedup plus magic-string removal in the
theurge crate (rbtd).

## Goal
The same single-home treatment for rbrv.env in Rust.

## Dependency
RBTDGC_RBRV_FILE is generated, not authored — it projects from the bash
RBCC_rbrv_file through rbcc_emit_consts (rbcc_Constants.sh), and rbtdgc_consts.rs
is marked "Do not edit." The bash constant is established by the bash rbrv
single-home pace (₢BbAAE), so this pace rides on it and inherits AAE's ₣BH gate
transitively — it is not independently doable before ₣BH despite carrying no gate
of its own.

## Locked
- Establish RBTDGC_RBRV_FILE by projection: ensure RBCC_rbrv_file is in the
  rbcc_emit_consts list and regenerate with tt/rbw-tb.Build.sh. Joins the
  RBTDGC_RBRN_FILE / RBRA_FILE / RBRO_FILE family. Never hand-edit the generated
  rbtdgc_consts.rs.
- Collapse the two ad-hoc local consts onto it — discover the `const ... =
  "rbrv.env"` definitions in rbtdro_onboarding.rs and rbtdrp_pristine.rs — and
  replace the bare `path.join("rbrv.env")` magic strings. Comments and
  error-format-strings keep the literal.

## Done
One shared RBTDGC_RBRV_FILE projected from rbcc; no local duplicate consts; no
bare path-join magic strings; tt/rbw-tb.Build.sh and tt/rbw-tt.Test.sh green.

### noun-cluster-crucible-family (₢BbAAC) [rough]

## Character
Stable-noun curation — terms are BH/BZ-safe so the docket is concrete, but
execution is file-gated on BH.

## Goal
Curate the crucible-runtime noun cluster (Crucible, Bottle, Sentry, Pentacle):
each traces to one canonical home (RBS0 quoin; RBYC fragment where
handbook-surfaced), and its drift-prone uses are made consistent — enrollment
descriptions and doc_briefs agree, no variant spellings, no in-source comment
re-definitions of what the noun means.

## Locked
- Curate, never evict — these nouns live. Removal of dead terms is the ₢BbAAB
  mechanism + BH's vocab scrub, not here.
- Target only drift-prone uses (cross-referencing descriptions, variant
  spellings, comment re-definitions); leave legitimate prose mentions alone.
- Apply ACG's reference-the-home discipline.

## Gate
After ₣BH (lode-universal-capture) lands — these descriptions live in
rbz_zipper.sh and the doc_briefs BH is rewriting.

## Done
Each cluster noun traces to one home; descriptions and doc_briefs consistent; no
variant spellings or in-source re-definitions survive; fast green.

### noun-cluster-vessel-identity (₢BbAAD) [rough]

## Character
Stable-noun curation — terms are BH/BZ-safe so the docket is concrete, but
execution is file-gated on BH.

## Goal
Curate the vessel-identity noun cluster (Vessel, Sigil, Nameplate): each traces
to one canonical home (RBS0 quoin; RBYC fragment where handbook-surfaced), and
its drift-prone uses are made consistent — enrollment descriptions and doc_briefs
agree, no variant spellings, no in-source comment re-definitions of what the noun
means. Watch the Vessel/Sigil boundary specifically (sigil = the per-vessel
directory name; vessel = the configured unit) so the two don't blur in prose.

## Locked
- Curate, never evict — these nouns live. Removal of dead terms is the ₢BbAAB
  mechanism + BH's vocab scrub, not here.
- Target only drift-prone uses (cross-referencing descriptions, variant
  spellings, comment re-definitions); leave legitimate prose mentions alone.
- Apply ACG's reference-the-home discipline.

## Gate
After ₣BH (lode-universal-capture) lands — these descriptions live in
rbz_zipper.sh and the doc_briefs BH is rewriting.

## Done
Each cluster noun traces to one home; descriptions and doc_briefs consistent; no
variant spellings or in-source re-definitions survive; fast green.

### axvo-symbol-lookahead (₢BbAAG) [rough]

## Character
MCM core-voicing amendment plus a voicing-wide mechanical migration. The mechanism is settled; one head fork sizes the migration.

## Goal
Make ACGm_102 (name-identity) mechanically decidable — give axvo_procedure / axvo_method an explicit implementing-symbol lookahead and carry it through the live operation voicings. Context: ACG "AXLA/MCM interface" section and ACGm_102's dependency note.

## Head fork (settle at mount)
Optional vs required. Required mirrors axvo_tabtarget's always-present sprue but migrates every live voicing and forbids the "no single symbol carries the link" exception (move-discipline rule 3); optional adds the symbol where wanted and keeps existing voicings conformant. Lean optional unless the exception proves empty in practice.

## Cinched
- One mcm_inlay = the literal implementing symbol as it appears in source — not mcm_sprue. A prefix-disciplined source symbol is an inlay; this matches axhems_scoped_method's existing read and mirrors axvo_tabtarget's literal lookahead.
- A backtick inlay is a distinct token type from a {...} attribute-reference, so it does not shift axd_grouped's "Nth linked term" counting.

## Gate
Discover live voicings: grep -rn "//axvo_procedure\|//axvo_method" across the S0 specs. The RBS0 share overlaps ₣BH's active rewrite surface — weigh running the RBS0 migration after ₣BH lands to avoid merge conflict.

## Done when
axvo_procedure / axvo_method carry the symbol lookahead with AXLA grammar prose updated; the chosen migration lands; the three S0 specs still validate; ACGm_102's dependency note flips to satisfied.

### buk-test-vocab-quoin-mint (₢BbAAN) [rough]

## Character
Minting — prefix-allocation judgment per the mint workflow, then small bounded linking.
Self-contained, single file.

## Goal
Home four under-quoined BUK test-framework concepts in BUS0
(Tools/buk/vov_veiled/BUS0-BashUtilitiesSpec.adoc): Fixture, Suite, Workbench, Testbench.
Mint a quoin for each — mapping entry, anchor, definition — then point the introducing sites at it.
Find sites: grep -i each word in BUS0.

## Cinched
Scope is exactly these four concepts in BUS0 — not a wider BUK mint sweep (operator grab-bag).
Running-prose saturation beyond the introducing sites rides future auto-link, not this pace
(see the link-coverage ledger entry in Memos/memo-20260209-diptych-vision.md).

## Done when
The four concepts carry quoins in BUS0's mapping section with definitions,
their introducing sites reference the quoins,
and BUS0 validates.

Mechanical sweep gated on one small design fork at its head — the first worked
application of ACG's magic-string discipline.

## Goal
Give the rbrv.env filename literal a single named home in bash; sweep the
path-construction sites onto it.

## Head fork (settle at mount)
Does the constant reference the existing rbrv_regime quoin (RBS0) or a freshly
minted filename quoin distinct from the regime? The regime-vs-file split is real
— sourcing/validation act on the regime; yoke/inscribe/enumerate act on the file
— so lean to a distinct filename quoin, but confirm against MCM "warrants
anchoring".

## Gate
After ₣BH (lode-universal-capture) lands. This sweep's path-construction sites
overlap BH's active rewrite surface — the inscribe cutover repoints the yoke and
Director-track rbrv.env handling, and BH's terminal vocab scrub greps the whole
tree last — so landing this in a parallel repo before BH's tail would guarantee a
merge conflict. Run it after BH is done, in the main line.

## Locked
- Constant joins the RBCC_rbr*_file family in rbcc_Constants.sh (sibling to
  RBCC_rbrn_file / rbra_file / rbro_file), with a breadcrumb to its spec quoin.
- Sweep only genuine path constructions. Discover: grep -rn "rbrv\.env" Tools/rbk
  --include="*.sh"; exclude prose — doc_params, buc_info/buh_line messages, and
  comments keep the literal (naming the file for a human is not a magic string).

## Done
Every bash path-construction resolves through the constant; prose literals
untouched; the constant carries its quoin breadcrumb; tt/rbw-tl.Shellcheck.sh and
tt/rbw-ts.TestSuite.fast.sh green.

### rbrv-env-rust-sweep (₢BbAAF) [rough]

## Character
Mechanical — RCG Constant Discipline: dedup plus magic-string removal in the
theurge crate (rbtd).

## Goal
The same single-home treatment for rbrv.env in Rust.

## Dependency
RBTDGC_RBRV_FILE is generated, not authored — it projects from the bash
RBCC_rbrv_file through rbcc_emit_consts (rbcc_Constants.sh), and rbtdgc_consts.rs
is marked "Do not edit." The bash constant is established by the bash rbrv
single-home pace (₢BbAAE), so this pace rides on it and inherits AAE's ₣BH gate
transitively — it is not independently doable before ₣BH despite carrying no gate
of its own.

## Locked
- Establish RBTDGC_RBRV_FILE by projection: ensure RBCC_rbrv_file is in the
  rbcc_emit_consts list and regenerate with tt/rbw-tb.Build.sh. Joins the
  RBTDGC_RBRN_FILE / RBRA_FILE / RBRO_FILE family. Never hand-edit the generated
  rbtdgc_consts.rs.
- Collapse the two ad-hoc local consts onto it — discover the `const ... =
  "rbrv.env"` definitions in rbtdro_onboarding.rs and rbtdrp_pristine.rs — and
  replace the bare `path.join("rbrv.env")` magic strings. Comments and
  error-format-strings keep the literal.

## Done
One shared RBTDGC_RBRV_FILE projected from rbcc; no local duplicate consts; no
bare path-join magic strings; tt/rbw-tb.Build.sh and tt/rbw-tt.Test.sh green.

# jjezs_paddock ₣Bb

## Shape

Pre-MVP source/spec hygiene grown into the ACG audit program, in two strata.
First, targeted allocation sweeps feathered in pace-at-a-time alongside MVP
feature finalization — magic-string -> constant sweeps and noun-cluster
curation, each with a blast radius that merges trivially.
Second, the audit program: per-pair spec/module audits under the ACG move
catalog, run while ACG is young so the guide and the documents harden together.
A seed, not a tree: scope stays tight to the source/spec allocation family.
Resist becoming the junk drawer for unrelated cleanups.

## Governing idea

The authority is ACG (Tools/cmk/vov_veiled/ACG-AllocationCodingGuide.md) —
the spine, the three homes, the move discipline, and the numbered move catalog
all live there.
This paddock cites and never restates
(ACG's statements -> definition-sites clause applied to itself;
an earlier revision of this paddock paraphrased the guide and has been drained).

## Cinched

- ACG = "Allocation Coding Guide" (not "Asciidoc" — the guide governs source
  authoring/allocation; asciidoc is MCM's domain).
  Veiled/proprietary, guide-family sibling to BCG/RCG/WSG/CBG.
  Not released at MVP.
- v1 posture: bless and name emergent practice; no mandated universals;
  no qualify-time enforcement.
  Universals are candidates confirmed pace-by-pace.
- Audit pace shape: one pace = one spec-subdocument/bash-module pair,
  source-side and spec-side moves applied in one read;
  the inherently cross-document moves (paraphrase collapse, tidemark drain)
  get single corpus-wide sweep paces instead.
- Pilot posture: ACG is untested — three pilots run before any roster of pair
  paces is slated.
  Every pilot's deliverable is twofold: the findings,
  and the ACG amendments where the guide proved vague, wrong, or silent.
  Roster vs further pilots is a post-pilot groom decision.
- Pilot selection (groom survey): the sentry pair tests the edit-time/Palisade
  comment boundary;
  the access-probe pair carries known-positive paraphrase/ornament specimens,
  so the audit proves itself against a known answer;
  the conjure pair is the richest surface and the scale test,
  gated with the rest of the ₣BH lane.
- Pilots execute at top model tier in the operator's chat;
  delegation to lesser tiers is for the post-pilot roster only,
  after ACG hardens.
- Antipattern catalog: mcm_antipattern broadens to cover delivery defects as
  well as document defects;
  Stun, Molehill, and word cancer all home in MCM's Antipatterns section,
  with ACG and the JJK gradient-delivery guidance citing rather than defining.

## Sequencing & lanes

Ungated front, in order: the antipattern-catalog pace, then the sentry pilot,
then the access-probe pilot —
the antipattern pace goes first because the access-probe pilot's word-cancer
findings cite the home it establishes.

The rest of the heat is gated on ₣BH (lode-universal-capture):

- Eviction (removing superseded terms) — ₣BH curates the KILL/KEEP list;
  the standing conformance engine's rows populate per-cluster behind ₣BH's
  cutovers.
  ACG names that engine the canonical home for evicted-term assertions.
- The conjure pilot — its surface is under active ₣BH rewrite;
  findings against it now would go stale.
- The rbrv.env single-home sweeps and the noun-cluster curation —
  their files share ₣BH's rewrite surface.
- Cluster repair (curating living vocabulary to a single home) is disjoint from
  eviction and never removes terms.

Triage rider across all audit work:
Manor / Depot / account-role vocabulary is ₣BZ's lane and
reliquary / Lode / Hallmark vocabulary is ₣BH's — report, never touch.
Verb clusters stay unslated until their owning heats land;
slating against terms two hot heats are still moving would re-create the drift
this heat exists to kill.

## Done looks like

ACG hardened through three audit pilots, amendments landed;
the antipattern catalog homed in MCM with citers repointed;
the rbrv.env literal single-homed in bash and rust as the first worked
ACGm_101 application;
the noun clusters curated to single homes;
the post-pilot groom has decided roster vs further pilots.

# jjezs_pace ₢BbAAC

## Character
Stable-noun curation — terms are BH/BZ-safe so the docket is concrete, but
execution is file-gated on BH.

## Goal
Curate the crucible-runtime noun cluster (Crucible, Bottle, Sentry, Pentacle):
each traces to one canonical home (RBS0 quoin; RBYC fragment where
handbook-surfaced), and its drift-prone uses are made consistent — enrollment
descriptions and doc_briefs agree, no variant spellings, no in-source comment
re-definitions of what the noun means.

## Locked
- Curate, never evict — these nouns live. Removal of dead terms is the ₢BbAAB
  mechanism + BH's vocab scrub, not here.
- Target only drift-prone uses (cross-referencing descriptions, variant
  spellings, comment re-definitions); leave legitimate prose mentions alone.
- Apply ACG's reference-the-home discipline.

## Gate
After ₣BH (lode-universal-capture) lands — these descriptions live in
rbz_zipper.sh and the doc_briefs BH is rewriting.

## Done
Each cluster noun traces to one home; descriptions and doc_briefs consistent; no
variant spellings or in-source re-definitions survive; fast green.

# jjezs_pace ₢BbAAD

## Character
Stable-noun curation — terms are BH/BZ-safe so the docket is concrete, but

## Goal

to one canonical home (RBS0 quoin; RBYC fragment where handbook-surfaced), and
its drift-prone uses are made consistent — enrollment descriptions and doc_briefs
agree, no variant spellings, no in-source comment re-definitions of what the noun
means. Watch the Vessel/Sigil boundary specifically (sigil = the per-vessel
directory name; vessel = the configured unit) so the two don't blur in prose.

## Locked
- Curate, never evict — these nouns live. Removal of dead terms is the ₢BbAAB
  mechanism + BH's vocab scrub, not here.
- Target only drift-prone uses (cross-referencing descriptions, variant
  spellings, comment re-definitions); leave legitimate prose mentions alone.
- Apply ACG's reference-the-home discipline.

## Gate
After ₣BH (lode-universal-capture) lands — these descriptions live in
rbz_zipper.sh and the doc_briefs BH is rewriting.

## Done
Each cluster noun traces to one home; descriptions and doc_briefs consistent; no
variant spellings or in-source re-definitions survive; fast green.

# jjezs_pace ₢BbAAE

## Character
Mechanical sweep gated on one small design fork at its head — the first worked
application of ACG's magic-string discipline.

## Goal
Give the rbrv.env filename literal a single named home in bash; sweep the
path-construction sites onto it.

## Head fork (settle at mount)
Does the constant reference the existing rbrv_regime quoin (RBS0) or a freshly
minted filename quoin distinct from the regime? The regime-vs-file split is real
— sourcing/validation act on the regime; yoke/inscribe/enumerate act on the file
— so lean to a distinct filename quoin, but confirm against MCM "warrants
anchoring".

## Gate
After ₣BH (lode-universal-capture) lands. This sweep's path-construction sites
overlap BH's active rewrite surface — the inscribe cutover repoints the yoke and
Director-track rbrv.env handling, and BH's terminal vocab scrub greps the whole
tree last — so landing this in a parallel repo before BH's tail would guarantee a
merge conflict. Run it after BH is done, in the main line.

## Locked
- Constant joins the RBCC_rbr*_file family in rbcc_Constants.sh (sibling to
  RBCC_rbrn_file / rbra_file / rbro_file), with a breadcrumb to its spec quoin.
- Sweep only genuine path constructions. Discover: grep -rn "rbrv\.env" Tools/rbk
  --include="*.sh"; exclude prose — doc_params, buc_info/buh_line messages, and
  comments keep the literal (naming the file for a human is not a magic string).

## Done
Every bash path-construction resolves through the constant; prose literals
untouched; the constant carries its quoin breadcrumb; tt/rbw-tl.Shellcheck.sh and
tt/rbw-ts.TestSuite.fast.sh green.

# jjezs_pace ₢BbAAF

## Character
Mechanical — RCG Constant Discipline: dedup plus magic-string removal in the
theurge crate (rbtd).

## Goal
The same single-home treatment for rbrv.env in Rust.

## Dependency
RBTDGC_RBRV_FILE is generated, not authored — it projects from the bash
RBCC_rbrv_file through rbcc_emit_consts (rbcc_Constants.sh), and rbtdgc_consts.rs
is marked "Do not edit." The bash constant is established by the bash rbrv
single-home pace (₢BbAAE), so this pace rides on it and inherits AAE's ₣BH gate
transitively — it is not independently doable before ₣BH despite carrying no gate
of its own.

## Locked
- Establish RBTDGC_RBRV_FILE by projection: ensure RBCC_rbrv_file is in the
  rbcc_emit_consts list and regenerate with tt/rbw-tb.Build.sh. Joins the
  RBTDGC_RBRN_FILE / RBRA_FILE / RBRO_FILE family. Never hand-edit the generated
  rbtdgc_consts.rs.
- Collapse the two ad-hoc local consts onto it — discover the `const ... =
  "rbrv.env"` definitions in rbtdro_onboarding.rs and rbtdrp_pristine.rs — and
  replace the bare `path.join("rbrv.env")` magic strings. Comments and
  error-format-strings keep the literal.

## Done
One shared RBTDGC_RBRV_FILE projected from rbcc; no local duplicate consts; no
bare path-join magic strings; tt/rbw-tb.Build.sh and tt/rbw-tt.Test.sh green.

# jjezs_pace ₢BbAAG

## Character
MCM core-voicing amendment plus a voicing-wide mechanical migration. The mechanism is settled; one head fork sizes the migration.

## Goal
Make ACGm_102 (name-identity) mechanically decidable — give axvo_procedure / axvo_method an explicit implementing-symbol lookahead and carry it through the live operation voicings. Context: ACG "AXLA/MCM interface" section and ACGm_102's dependency note.

## Head fork (settle at mount)
Optional vs required. Required mirrors axvo_tabtarget's always-present sprue but migrates every live voicing and forbids the "no single symbol carries the link" exception (move-discipline rule 3); optional adds the symbol where wanted and keeps existing voicings conformant. Lean optional unless the exception proves empty in practice.

## Cinched
- One mcm_inlay = the literal implementing symbol as it appears in source — not mcm_sprue. A prefix-disciplined source symbol is an inlay; this matches axhems_scoped_method's existing read and mirrors axvo_tabtarget's literal lookahead.
- A backtick inlay is a distinct token type from a {...} attribute-reference, so it does not shift axd_grouped's "Nth linked term" counting.

## Gate
Discover live voicings: grep -rn "//axvo_procedure\|//axvo_method" across the S0 specs. The RBS0 share overlaps ₣BH's active rewrite surface — weigh running the RBS0 migration after ₣BH lands to avoid merge conflict.

## Done when
axvo_procedure / axvo_method carry the symbol lookahead with AXLA grammar prose updated; the chosen migration lands; the three S0 specs still validate; ACGm_102's dependency note flips to satisfied.

# jjezs_pace ₢BbAAH

## Character
Small MCM/ACG/JJK cross-document curation — one definitional broadening, three quoin mints, citer repointing.

## Goal
Make MCM's Antipatterns section the single catalog for named recurring defects,
document-side and delivery-side alike;
home Stun, Molehill, and word cancer there as quoins;
flip every defining citer to cite.

## Cinched
- mcm_antipattern's definition broadens from document defect to also cover
  delivery defects (operator-facing presentation failures) — the junk-drawer
  election, operator-cinched at groom.
- Word cancer mirrors the tidemark pattern: concept home is the MCM quoin;
  ACG keeps the moves (statements clause, ACGm_105, registry) and cites.
- Stun and Molehill keep their operational guidance in the JJK
  gradient-delivery section (claude-jjk-bhyslop.md), which cites the MCM home
  instead of defining.

## Done when
MCM Antipatterns carries the broadened definition plus quoins for word cancer,
Stun, and Molehill; ACG and the JJK gradient-delivery guidance cite rather than
define; MCM validates.

# jjezs_pace ₢BbAAI

## Character
First ACG audit pilot — detect-only judgment work; the guide is on trial as much
as the documents.

## Gate
Top-tier model only (Fable-class at slating), worked in the operator's chat —
never mount or delegate this pace at a lesser tier.
The pilot's primary deliverable is ACG amendment judgment — noticing where the
guide is vague, wrong, or silent — and delegation makes that feedback
secondhand.
If mounted on a lesser tier, stop and tell the operator instead of proceeding.

## Goal

ACG's detect-only moves (ACGm_104 conceptual-comment eviction, ACGm_106 ornament
drain; note ACGm_105/107 findings in passing for the corpus sweeps).
Twin deliverable: the findings report,
and ACG amendments wherever the guide proved vague, wrong, or silent —
pilots harden the guide.

## Cinched
- Per-pair shape: source side and spec side read together; a relocation's
  receiving quoin is judged against this pair's spec.
- Detect-only licensing per ACG: report findings; relocations land one reviewed
  move at a time, never batch.
- This pair's dense security-rationale comments are the deliberate test of the
  edit-time/Palisade boundary — whether why-comments at the iptables/conntrack
  sites are evictable design-time knowledge or legitimate Palisade
  characterization. Settle with the operator; record the ruling as an ACG
  amendment.

## Done when
Findings report exists with each finding tagged by move-type and receiving home;
agreed relocations/drains landed as individually reviewed moves;
ACG amendments landed;
fast suite green if any source file changed.

# jjezs_pace ₢BbAAJ

specimens.

## Gate

## Goal

RBSAJ + RBSAO — under ACG's detect-only moves, with emphasis on ACGm_105

the groom survey found spec and source wording the bounded

and rationale-as-normative prose in RBSAJ.

## Cinched

- Triage rider: Manor/Payor/account-role vocabulary is ₣BZ's lane — report,
  never touch.
- Word-cancer findings cite the MCM antipattern home established by the
  preceding antipattern-catalog pace.

## Done when
Findings report with move-type tags;
agreed collapses/drains landed as individually reviewed moves,
one authoritative wording per idea with citations elsewhere;
ACG amendments landed;
fast suite green if any source file changed.

# jjezs_pace ₢BbAAK

pairing.

Two gates, both hard:

- Top-tier model only (Fable-class at slating), worked in the operator's chat —
  never mount or delegate this pace at a lesser tier.
  The pilot's primary deliverable is ACG amendment judgment plus the roster
  recommendation, and delegation makes that feedback secondhand.
  If mounted on a lesser tier, stop and tell the operator instead of proceeding.
- After ₣BH (lode-universal-capture) lands — the conjure surface is under
  active ₣BH rewrite (ensconce cutover, anchor wiring);
  findings against it now go stale.

## Goal
Audit the conjure pair — Tools/rbk/rbfd_FoundryDirectorBuild.sh (plus its
rbfca_/rbfcg_/rbfcp_ support where the spec reaches) against RBSAC — under the
full detect-only move set.
This pilot answers whether the per-pair shape scales:
biggest source surface, deep conceptual comments
(anchor semantics, egress-mode branching, two-layer preflight).
Twin deliverable: findings plus ACG amendments,
closing with a recommendation for the post-pilot groom —
roster of pair paces vs further pilots.

## Cinched
- Runs only after the first two pilots' ACG amendments have landed — this pilot
  exercises the hardened guide.
- Reliquary/Lode/Hallmark vocabulary belongs to ₣BH's lane even after it lands;
  term curation stays out of scope — allocation findings only.

## Done when
Findings report with move-type tags;
agreed moves landed as individually reviewed moves;
ACG amendments landed;
roster recommendation recorded for the post-pilot groom;
fast suite green if any source file changed.

# jjezs_pace ₢BbAAN

Minting — prefix-allocation judgment per the mint workflow, then small bounded linking.
Self-contained, single file.

## Goal
Home four under-quoined BUK test-framework concepts in BUS0

(see the link-coverage ledger entry in Memos/memo-20260209-diptych-vision.md).

## Done when
The four concepts carry quoins in BUS0's mapping section with definitions,
their introducing sites reference the quoins,
and BUS0 validates.

₣Bb (rbk-10-mvp-acg-scrub) is racing with 2 paces landed and 10 remaining: an ungated front of three (antipattern catalog → sentry pilot → access-probe pilot), then a ₣BH-gated block, with two stragglers at the tail. The dockets are in good shape overall — the study found one systematic inconsistency, a cluster of stale file paths, and one ordering wrinkle. On models: yes, I'd differentiate — detail below.

## Consistency findings

**1. Heading drift across the slating generations.** Four paces — ₢BbAAC, ₢BbAAD, ₢BbAAE, ₢BbAAF — use `## Locked` and bare `## Done`, both of which the JJK docket discipline now explicitly bars (`## Cinched` / `## Done when`). The six newer paces use the current headings. A mechanical mass-reslate fixes this.

**2. Stale paths — verified against the tree, not guessed.** Classic stale-by-mount drift; a mounting agent would grep into a wall:
- ₢BbAAI cites `Tools/rbk/rbjs_sentry.sh` — the file now lives at `rbmm_moorings/rbmv_vessels/common-sentry-context/rbjs_sentry.sh`. (The RBJS entry in `claude-rbk-acronyms.md` is stale too — same wrong path. Separate small fix, outside this heat.)
- ₢BbAAJ cites `rbgv_AccessProbe.sh` — now `rbgv_probe.sh`.
- ₢BbAAK cites `rbfd_FoundryDirectorBuild.sh` — now `rbfd_director.sh`.
- ₢BbAAE cites `rbcc_Constants.sh` — now `rbcc_constants.sh` (case only; minor).
- ₢BbAAF, by contrast, checks out exactly: both ad-hoc consts still exist as docketed in `rbtdrp_pristine.rs` and `rbtdro_onboarding.rs`.

The durable fix is reslating these to acronym references (RBJS, RBGV, RBFD) rather than fresher paths — acronyms survive renames.

**3. Coronet refs in prose.** ₢BbAAC/₢BbAAD cite "the ₢BbAAB mechanism" (a landed pace) and ₢BbAAF cites ₢BbAAE, its immediate predecessor. Reference discipline says refer by purpose; ₢BbAAF's is the most defensible (it explains transitive gate inheritance), but all three read better as "the standing conformance-engine eviction mechanism" / "the bash single-home pace." Light prune while reslating anyway.

**4. ₢BbAAG's `## Gate` is mislabeled.** Its content is a discovery recipe plus a soft "weigh running the RBS0 share after ₣BH" — not a hard gate. Worth either splitting (discovery into Goal, an honest "partially gated: RBS0 share only" note) or accepting it runs in the ungated front with the RBS0 migration deferred.

**5. Ordering wrinkle.** ₢BbAAN (buk-test-vocab-quoin-mint) is ungated and fully self-contained — single BUK file, no ₣BH overlap — yet sits dead last behind the entire gated block. It belongs in the ungated front; a rail moving it (and arguably ₢BbAAG, given finding 4) ahead of ₢BbAAK would make heat order match actionability.

Otherwise: the paddock and dockets agree with each other, the pilot gates are uniform and consistent with the paddock cinch, and ₢BbAAC/₢BbAAD's parallel structure is deliberate and clean.

## Model advice

Yes — the heat splits naturally into judgment work and mechanical work, and running it all at one tier wastes one direction or the other:

| Pace | Tier | Why |
|------|------|-----|
| ₢BbAAI, ₢BbAAJ, ₢BbAAK (pilots) | Fable, operator chat | Already hard-gated and cinched in the paddock — no change. |
| ₢BbAAH (antipattern catalog) | Fable/Opus, operator chat | Small but definitional — it broadens an MCM core term and mints the home the pilots cite. Since it leads the front you'll work in this chat anyway. |
| ₢BbAAG (axvo lookahead), ₢BbAAN (quoin mint) | Opus | MCM core-voicing amendment and prefix-allocation minting — judgment you care about, not mechanics. |
| ₢BbAAC, ₢BbAAD (noun curation) | Opus | The "drift-prone use vs legitimate prose mention" call is taste-heavy; a lesser tier will over-fix exactly the mentions the cinch says to leave alone. |
| ₢BbAAE (bash sweep) | Sonnet | Mechanical once the head fork settles; the fork has a documented lean the operator confirms at mount. |
| ₢BbAAF (rust sweep) | Sonnet (Haiku viable) | The most mechanical pace in the heat — two named consts, a projection step, build+test verification. Sonnet only because the regenerate-don't-hand-edit step punishes carelessness. |

If you want this durable rather than remembered, I can add a one-line tier note to the sweep dockets during the same reslate — the pilots already establish the precedent of recording tier in the docket when it matters.

## Proposed operations

1. **Mass reslate** (₢BbAAC, ₢BbAAD, ₢BbAAE, ₢BbAAF, ₢BbAAI, ₢BbAAJ, ₢BbAAK, ₢BbAAG): heading normalization, stale-path → acronym repointing, coronet prune, AAG gate clarification, optional tier notes.
2. **Rail** ₢BbAAN (and ₢BbAAG if you accept finding 4) ahead of ₢BbAAK.
3. **Side fix**: correct the RBJS path in `claude-rbk-acronyms.md` — one line, heat-affiliated notch.

Want all three, a subset, or shall we look at any finding more closely first?

# jjezs_reslate ₢BbAAC

## Character
Stable-noun curation — terms are BH/BZ-safe so the docket is concrete,
but execution is file-gated on BH.
Tier: opus — the drift-vs-legitimate-mention call is judgment, not mechanics.

## Goal
Curate the crucible-runtime noun cluster (Crucible, Bottle, Sentry, Pentacle):
each traces to one canonical home (RBS0 quoin; RBYC fragment where
handbook-surfaced),
and its drift-prone uses are made consistent —
enrollment descriptions and doc_briefs agree, no variant spellings,
no in-source comment re-definitions of what the noun means.

## Cinched
- Curate, never evict — these nouns live.
  Removal of dead terms is the standing conformance-engine eviction mechanism
  plus BH's vocab scrub, not here.
- Target only drift-prone uses (cross-referencing descriptions, variant
  spellings, comment re-definitions); leave legitimate prose mentions alone.
- Apply ACG's reference-the-home discipline.

## Gate
After ₣BH (lode-universal-capture) lands — these descriptions live in
rbz_zipper.sh and the doc_briefs BH is rewriting.

## Done when
Each cluster noun traces to one home;
descriptions and doc_briefs consistent;
no variant spellings or in-source re-definitions survive;
fast green.

# jjezs_reslate ₢BbAAD

## Character
Stable-noun curation — terms are BH/BZ-safe so the docket is concrete,
but execution is file-gated on BH.
Tier: opus — the drift-vs-legitimate-mention call is judgment, not mechanics.

## Goal
Curate the vessel-identity noun cluster (Vessel, Sigil, Nameplate):
each traces to one canonical home (RBS0 quoin; RBYC fragment where
handbook-surfaced),
and its drift-prone uses are made consistent —
enrollment descriptions and doc_briefs agree, no variant spellings,
no in-source comment re-definitions of what the noun means.
Watch the Vessel/Sigil boundary specifically
(sigil = the per-vessel directory name; vessel = the configured unit)
so the two don't blur in prose.

## Cinched
- Curate, never evict — these nouns live.
  Removal of dead terms is the standing conformance-engine eviction mechanism
  plus BH's vocab scrub, not here.
- Target only drift-prone uses (cross-referencing descriptions, variant
  spellings, comment re-definitions); leave legitimate prose mentions alone.

fast green.

# jjezs_reslate ₢BbAAE

## Character
Mechanical sweep gated on one small design fork at its head —
the first worked application of ACG's magic-string discipline.
Tier: sonnet — mechanical once the head fork settles;
the fork carries a documented lean for the operator to confirm at mount.

## Goal
Give the rbrv.env filename literal a single named home in bash;
sweep the path-construction sites onto it.

## Head fork (settle at mount)
Does the constant reference the existing rbrv_regime quoin (RBS0)
or a freshly minted filename quoin distinct from the regime?
The regime-vs-file split is real —
sourcing/validation act on the regime; yoke/inscribe/enumerate act on the file —
so lean to a distinct filename quoin,
but confirm against MCM "warrants anchoring".

## Gate
After ₣BH (lode-universal-capture) lands.
This sweep's path-construction sites overlap BH's active rewrite surface —
the inscribe cutover repoints the yoke and Director-track rbrv.env handling,
and BH's terminal vocab scrub greps the whole tree last —
so landing this in a parallel repo before BH's tail would guarantee a merge
conflict.
Run it after BH is done, in the main line.

## Cinched
- Constant joins the RBCC_rbr*_file family in rbcc_constants.sh
  (sibling to RBCC_rbrn_file / rbra_file / rbro_file),
  with a breadcrumb to its spec quoin.
- Sweep only genuine path constructions.
  Discover: grep -rn "rbrv\.env" Tools/rbk --include="*.sh";
  exclude prose — doc_params, buc_info/buh_line messages, and comments keep the
  literal (naming the file for a human is not a magic string).

## Done when
Every bash path-construction resolves through the constant;
prose literals untouched;
the constant carries its quoin breadcrumb;
tt/rbw-tl.Shellcheck.sh and tt/rbw-ts.TestSuite.fast.sh green.

# jjezs_reslate ₢BbAAF

## Character
Mechanical — RCG Constant Discipline:
dedup plus magic-string removal in the theurge crate (rbtd).
Tier: sonnet (haiku viable) —
the regenerate-don't-hand-edit projection step is the only care point.

## Goal
The same single-home treatment for rbrv.env in Rust.

## Dependency
RBTDGC_RBRV_FILE is generated, not authored —
it projects from the bash RBCC_rbrv_file through rbcc_emit_consts
(rbcc_constants.sh), and rbtdgc_consts.rs is marked "Do not edit."
The bash constant is established by the bash rbrv single-home pace immediately
preceding,
so this pace rides on it and inherits that pace's ₣BH gate transitively —
it is not independently doable before ₣BH despite carrying no gate of its own.

## Cinched
- Establish RBTDGC_RBRV_FILE by projection:
  ensure RBCC_rbrv_file is in the rbcc_emit_consts list and regenerate with
  tt/rbw-tb.Build.sh.
  Joins the RBTDGC_RBRN_FILE / RBRA_FILE / RBRO_FILE family.
  Never hand-edit the generated rbtdgc_consts.rs.
- Collapse the two ad-hoc local consts onto it —
  discover the `const ... = "rbrv.env"` definitions in rbtdro_onboarding.rs and
  rbtdrp_pristine.rs —
  and replace the bare `path.join("rbrv.env")` magic strings.
  Comments and error-format-strings keep the literal.

## Done when
One shared RBTDGC_RBRV_FILE projected from rbcc;
no local duplicate consts;
no bare path-join magic strings;
tt/rbw-tb.Build.sh and tt/rbw-tt.Test.sh green.

# jjezs_reslate ₢BbAAG

## Character
MCM core-voicing amendment plus a voicing-wide mechanical migration.
The mechanism is settled; one head fork sizes the migration.
Tier: opus — core-voicing grammar judgment.

## Goal
Make ACGm_102 (name-identity) mechanically decidable —
give axvo_procedure / axvo_method an explicit implementing-symbol lookahead
and carry it through the live operation voicings.
Context: ACG "AXLA/MCM interface" section and ACGm_102's dependency note.
Discover live voicings:
grep -rn "//axvo_procedure\|//axvo_method" across the S0 specs.

## Head fork (settle at mount)
Optional vs required.
Required mirrors axvo_tabtarget's always-present sprue
but migrates every live voicing and forbids the
"no single symbol carries the link" exception (move-discipline rule 3);
optional adds the symbol where wanted and keeps existing voicings conformant.
Lean optional unless the exception proves empty in practice.

## Cinched
- One mcm_inlay = the literal implementing symbol as it appears in source —
  not mcm_sprue.
  A prefix-disciplined source symbol is an inlay;
  this matches axhems_scoped_method's existing read and mirrors
  axvo_tabtarget's literal lookahead.
- A backtick inlay is a distinct token type from a {...} attribute-reference,
  so it does not shift axd_grouped's "Nth linked term" counting.

## Gate
Partial.
The AXLA grammar amendment and the JJS0/VOS0 voicing migrations are ungated;
the RBS0 share of the migration overlaps ₣BH's active rewrite surface and
defers until ₣BH lands.

## Done when
axvo_procedure / axvo_method carry the symbol lookahead with AXLA grammar prose
updated;
the chosen migration lands;
the three S0 specs still validate;
ACGm_102's dependency note flips to satisfied.

# jjezs_reslate ₢BbAAI

## Character
First ACG audit pilot — detect-only judgment work;
the guide is on trial as much as the documents.

## Gate
Top-tier model only (Fable-class at slating), worked in the operator's chat —
never mount or delegate this pace at a lesser tier.
The pilot's primary deliverable is ACG amendment judgment —
noticing where the guide is vague, wrong, or silent —
and delegation makes that feedback secondhand.
If mounted on a lesser tier, stop and tell the operator instead of proceeding.

## Goal
Audit the sentry pair —
RBJS (rbjs_sentry.sh, shipped in the common-sentry-context vessel build
context) against RBSSR + RBSSS —
under ACG's detect-only moves
(ACGm_104 conceptual-comment eviction, ACGm_106 ornament drain;
note ACGm_105/107 findings in passing for the corpus sweeps).
Twin deliverable: the findings report,
and ACG amendments wherever the guide proved vague, wrong, or silent —
pilots harden the guide.

## Cinched
- Per-pair shape: source side and spec side read together;
  a relocation's receiving quoin is judged against this pair's spec.
- Detect-only licensing per ACG: report findings;
  relocations land one reviewed move at a time, never batch.
- This pair's dense security-rationale comments are the deliberate test of the
  edit-time/Palisade boundary —
  whether why-comments at the iptables/conntrack sites are evictable
  design-time knowledge or legitimate Palisade characterization.
  Settle with the operator; record the ruling as an ACG amendment.

## Done when
Findings report exists with each finding tagged by move-type and receiving home;
agreed relocations/drains landed as individually reviewed moves;
ACG amendments landed;
fast suite green if any source file changed.

# jjezs_reslate ₢BbAAJ

## Character
Second ACG audit pilot — prose-judgment focus;
runs against known-positive specimens.

## Gate
Top-tier model only (Fable-class at slating), worked in the operator's chat —
never mount or delegate this pace at a lesser tier.
The pilot's primary deliverable is ACG amendment judgment —
noticing where the guide is vague, wrong, or silent —
and delegation makes that feedback secondhand.
If mounted on a lesser tier, stop and tell the operator instead of proceeding.

## Goal
Audit the access-probe pair —
RBGV (Tools/rbk/rbgv_probe.sh) against RBSAJ + RBSAO —
under ACG's detect-only moves, with emphasis on ACGm_105 (paraphrase collapse)
and ACGm_106 (ornament drain):
the groom survey found spec and source wording the bounded
propagation-absorbing-retry idea twice,
and rationale-as-normative prose in RBSAJ.
Twin deliverable: findings plus ACG amendments.

## Cinched
- The known specimens serve as ACG rule-2 known answers:
  if the audit misses them, the detect rule is wrong —
  fix the guide, not the report.
- Triage rider: Manor/Payor/account-role vocabulary is ₣BZ's lane —
  report, never touch.
- Word-cancer findings cite the MCM antipattern home established by the
  preceding antipattern-catalog pace.

## Done when
Findings report with move-type tags;
agreed collapses/drains landed as individually reviewed moves,
one authoritative wording per idea with citations elsewhere;
ACG amendments landed;
fast suite green if any source file changed.

# jjezs_reslate ₢BbAAK

## Character
Third ACG audit pilot — the scale test on the richest surface;
multi-module pairing.

## Gate
Two gates, both hard:

- Top-tier model only (Fable-class at slating), worked in the operator's chat —
  never mount or delegate this pace at a lesser tier.
  The pilot's primary deliverable is ACG amendment judgment plus the roster
  recommendation, and delegation makes that feedback secondhand.
  If mounted on a lesser tier, stop and tell the operator instead of proceeding.
- After ₣BH (lode-universal-capture) lands —
  the conjure surface is under active ₣BH rewrite
  (ensconce cutover, anchor wiring);
  findings against it now go stale.

## Goal
Audit the conjure pair —
RBFD (Tools/rbk/rbfd_director.sh, plus its rbfca_/rbfcg_/rbfcp_ support where
the spec reaches) against RBSAC —
under the full detect-only move set.
This pilot answers whether the per-pair shape scales:
biggest source surface, deep conceptual comments
(anchor semantics, egress-mode branching, two-layer preflight).
Twin deliverable: findings plus ACG amendments,
closing with a recommendation for the post-pilot groom —
roster of pair paces vs further pilots.

## Cinched
- Runs only after the first two pilots' ACG amendments have landed —
  this pilot exercises the hardened guide.
- Reliquary/Lode/Hallmark vocabulary belongs to ₣BH's lane even after it lands;
  term curation stays out of scope — allocation findings only.

## Done when
Findings report with move-type tags;
agreed moves landed as individually reviewed moves;
ACG amendments landed;
roster recommendation recorded for the post-pilot groom;
fast suite green if any source file changed.

Optional vs required.

# jjezs_paddock ₣Bb

## Shape

Pre-MVP source/spec hygiene grown into the ACG audit program, in two strata.
First, targeted allocation sweeps feathered in pace-at-a-time alongside MVP
feature finalization — magic-string -> constant sweeps and noun-cluster
curation, each with a blast radius that merges trivially.
Second, the audit program: per-pair spec/module audits under the ACG move
catalog, run while ACG is young so the guide and the documents harden together.
A seed, not a tree: scope stays tight to the source/spec allocation family.
Resist becoming the junk drawer for unrelated cleanups.

## Governing idea

The authority is ACG (Tools/cmk/vov_veiled/ACG-AllocationCodingGuide.md) —
the spine, the three homes, the move discipline, and the numbered move catalog
all live there.
This paddock cites and never restates
(ACG's statements -> definition-sites clause applied to itself;
an earlier revision of this paddock paraphrased the guide and has been drained).

## Cinched

- ACG = "Allocation Coding Guide" (not "Asciidoc" — the guide governs source
  authoring/allocation; asciidoc is MCM's domain).
  Veiled/proprietary, guide-family sibling to BCG/RCG/WSG/CBG.
  Not released at MVP.
- v1 posture: bless and name emergent practice; no mandated universals;
  no qualify-time enforcement.
  Universals are candidates confirmed pace-by-pace.
- Audit pace shape: one pace = one spec-subdocument/bash-module pair,
  source-side and spec-side moves applied in one read;
  the inherently cross-document moves (paraphrase collapse, tidemark drain)
  get single corpus-wide sweep paces instead.
- Pilot posture: ACG is untested — three pilots run before any roster of pair
  paces is slated.
  Every pilot's deliverable is twofold: the findings,
  and the ACG amendments where the guide proved vague, wrong, or silent.
  Roster vs further pilots is a post-pilot groom decision.
- Pilot selection (groom survey): the sentry pair tests the edit-time/Palisade
  comment boundary;
  the access-probe pair carries known-positive paraphrase/ornament specimens,
  so the audit proves itself against a known answer;
  the conjure pair is the richest surface and the scale test,
  gated with the rest of the ₣BH lane.
- Pilots execute at top model tier in the operator's chat;
  delegation to lesser tiers is for the post-pilot roster only,
  after ACG hardens.
- Per-pace model tier rides in each docket's Character line
  (judgment/curation work at opus, the mechanical sweeps at sonnet);
  the pilot top-tier gate stays a hard Gate, not a Character hint.
- Antipattern catalog: mcm_antipattern broadens to cover delivery defects as
  well as document defects;
  Stun, Molehill, and word cancer all home in MCM's Antipatterns section,
  with ACG and the JJK gradient-delivery guidance citing rather than defining.

## Sequencing & lanes

Ungated front, in order: the antipattern-catalog pace, then the sentry pilot,
then the access-probe pilot —
the antipattern pace goes first because the access-probe pilot's word-cancer
findings cite the home it establishes.
The BUK test-vocab mint and the axvo symbol-lookahead amendment ride the back
of the ungated front:
the mint is self-contained in BUS0,
and the axvo pace is only partially gated —
its RBS0 migration share defers until ₣BH lands.

The rest of the heat is gated on ₣BH:

- Eviction (removing superseded terms) — ₣BH curates the KILL/KEEP list;
  the standing conformance engine's rows populate per-cluster behind ₣BH's
  cutovers.
  ACG names that engine the canonical home for evicted-term assertions.
- The conjure pilot — its surface is under active ₣BH rewrite;
  findings against it now would go stale.
- The rbrv.env single-home sweeps and the noun-cluster curation —
  their files share ₣BH's rewrite surface.
- Cluster repair (curating living vocabulary to a single home) is disjoint from
  eviction and never removes terms.

Triage rider across all audit work:
Manor / Depot / account-role vocabulary is ₣BZ's lane and
reliquary / Lode / Hallmark vocabulary is ₣BH's — report, never touch.
Verb clusters stay unslated until their owning heats land;
slating against terms two hot heats are still moving would re-create the drift
this heat exists to kill.

## Done looks like

ACG hardened through three audit pilots, amendments landed;
the antipattern catalog homed in MCM with citers repointed;
the rbrv.env literal single-homed in bash and rust as the first worked
ACGm_101 application;
the noun clusters curated to single homes;
the post-pilot groom has decided roster vs further pilots.

Owns no kind knowledge — capture-domain constants (mason SA, TETHER pool, regime timeout) are spine-owned, the kind-flavored poll ceiling is passed by the body. 48	  - **RBLDB** → `rbk/rbldb_*.sh` (Bole body — base-kind ensconce, thinned to ride the spine: owns only the ensconce recipe (gcrane capture + docker vouch push), the substitutions blob, and the per-member `slot_n` extract loop, composing through `zrbld_spine_dispatch`/`zrbld_spine_extract`. Holds `zrbld_ensconce_submit`/`_extract`/`rbld_ensconce`; no build-submission or step-composition machinery.)
49	  - **RBLDL** → `rbk/rbldl_*.sh` (Lode lifecycle REST — `divine`/`banish`, direct GAR-REST host ops, kept distinct from the assembly spine. Guard-free cluster sourced by `rbld0_lode`.)
